summary: v1.11.1 manifest-sync regression testing — verified fix for stale companion/manifest.json version that prevented Companion from refreshing variable/action/feedback definitions across module upgrades. All 21 manifest-sync assertions pass, all 6 legacy test suites pass (203/203 assertions), tarball is correctly built with matching manifest, and sync-manifest npm script works as a tamper-proof regression guard.

independent_verifications.tarball_bundled_main_js_literals: all 5 required strings present (exposure_compensation, iris_fstop, focus_percent, zoom_percent, backlight) + bonus (pan_degrees, tilt_degrees)
independent_verifications.sync_manifest_tamper_test: manually set manifest.version to '9.9.9-tamper', ran `npm run sync-manifest`, verified restored to 1.11.1, then re-restored from backup
independent_verifications.npm_availability: npm 10.8.2 on PATH, node v20.20.2 — execSync works

critical_code_review_comments: sync-manifest script is written as an inline `node -e` one-liner using JSON.parse/writeFileSync — works, but for readability/testability, consider extracting to `scripts/sync-manifest.mjs`. Not critical since manifest-sync.test.js already guards behavior.

The package.json inline script uses `require('fs')` inside `node -e` from an ESM package ("type": "module"). This currently works because `node -e` is evaluated in CJS mode by default, but is worth noting for future Node versions. Verified working on Node v20.20.2.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v1.11.1 fixes stale companion/manifest.json version — the root cause of new variables (exposure_compensation, iris_fstop, etc.) not appearing in Companion after module upgrade. `npm run sync-manifest` now runs automatically before every build and is regression-guarded by test/manifest-sync.test.js. Any future version bump should only touch package.json.version — the build script will handle manifest.version. If manifest.json version diverges from package.json.version in a future iteration, the CI test will fail.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: Companion caches module definitions (variables/actions/feedbacks) keyed by manifest.version. When a user upgrades the .tgz but the manifest version string is unchanged, Companion skips re-registering definitions, so newly added variables never appear in the UI. The manifest.json had been stuck at '1.3.2' across 8 package.json bumps. Fix (v1.11.1): (1) manually bumped companion/manifest.json to 1.11.1, (2) added `npm run sync-manifest` that copies package.version → manifest.version, (3) wired `npm run build` to run sync-manifest before packaging, (4) fixed `npm run dev` to pass --dev flag. All independent checks confirmed by this test run.
